2603.21520.title: Generalizable Self-Evolving Memory for Automatic Prompt Optimization
2603.21520.paper_summary: Automatic prompt optimization is a promising approach for adapting large language models (LLMs) to downstream tasks, yet existing methods typically search for a specific prompt specialized to a fixed task. This paradigm limits generalization across heterogeneous queries and prevents models from accumulating reusable prompting knowledge over time. In this paper, we propose MemAPO, a memory-driven framework that reconceptualizes prompt optimization as generalizable and self-evolving experience accumulation. MemAPO maintains a dual-memory mechanism that distills successful reasoning trajectories into reusable strategy templates while organizing incorrect generations into structured error patterns that capture recurrent failure modes. Given a new prompt, the framework retrieves both relevant strategies and failure patterns to compose prompts that promote effective reasoning while discouraging known mistakes. Through iterative self-reflection and memory editing, MemAPO continuously updates its memory, enabling prompt optimization to improve over time rather than restarting from scratch for each task. Experiments on diverse benchmarks show that MemAPO consistently outperforms representative prompt optimization baselines while substantially reducing optimization cost.
2603.21520.paper_summary_zh: 自動提示優化是一種有前景的方法，用於將大型語言模型（LLMs）適應於下游任務，但現有的方法通常尋找專門針對固定任務的特定提示。這種範式限制了在異質查詢之間的泛化，並阻止模型隨著時間的推移積累可重用的提示知識。在本文中，我們提出了MemAPO，一個以記憶為驅動的框架，將提示優化重新概念化為可泛化和自我演變的經驗積累。MemAPO維護一種雙記憶機制，將成功的推理軌跡提煉為可重用的策略模板，同時將不正確的生成組織成結構化的錯誤模式，以捕捉重複的失敗模式。給定一個新的提示，該框架檢索相關的策略和失敗模式，以組合促進有效推理的提示，同時避免已知的錯誤。通過迭代自我反思和記憶編輯，MemAPO不斷更新其記憶，使提示優化隨著時間的推移而改進，而不是對每個任務從頭開始。對多樣基準的實驗表明，MemAPO始終超越代表性的提示優化基準，同時大幅降低優化成本。
2603.21520.author: Guanbao Liang et.al.
2603.21520.authors: Guanbao Liang, Yuanchen Bei, Sheng Zhou, Yuheng Qin, Huan Zhou, Bingxin Jia, Bin Li, Jiajun Bu
